description: Ambawalage Sujeewa Priyadarshani Silva Vs. Debt Conciliation Board and Others Ambawalage Sujeewa Priyadarshani Silva Vs. Debt Conciliation Board and Others View More before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: Ambawalage Sujeewa Priyadarshani Silva Vs. Debt Conciliation Board and Others Ambawalage Sujeewa Priyadarshani Silva Vs. Debt Conciliation Board and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner made an application to the Debt Conciliation Board to have a debt settled on the basis that the Deed of Transfer on the face of it was only executed to secure a mortgage, which the Board declined after inquiry. The Petitioner invoked the jurisdiction of this Court challenging the same. Held – Since the Petitioner never parted with the possession of the land in suit after signing of the impugned deed, never intended to transfer the beneficial interest, and the huge disparity between the value of the property in suit and the consideration passed, writs issued as prayed for as the Board had erred in facts and in law.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka

description: S Potencia L.L.C-FZ Petitioner Vs. Lanka Coal Company (Pvt) Ltd and Others S Potencia L.L.C-FZ Petitioner Vs. Lanka Coal Company (Pvt) Ltd and Others View More before Hon.Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: S Potencia L.L.C-FZ Petitioner Vs. Lanka Coal Company (Pvt) Ltd and Others S Potencia L.L.C-FZ Petitioner Vs. Lanka Coal Company (Pvt) Ltd and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon.Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner is a foreign company who had entered into a contract with the 1st Respondent (Government-owned company) for the purpose of supplying coal to the Norochcholai Power Plant. The main agreement contained an arbitral agreement; however, despite the arbitral agreement, the Petitioner sought to invoke the writ jurisdiction of this Court to quash the cancellation of the contract entered into between the parties. Held – Court does not have jurisdiction when an arbitral clause exists as per Section 5 of the Arbitration Act, No. 11 of 1995; Application dismissed without cost.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka
